places.name: The Long Room Library at Trinity College
places.description: The gorgeous library at Trinity College is home to remarkable treasures of Irish identity.

places.name: St. Michan's Mummies
places.description: An Irish church where an 800-year-old mummy is reaching out of his coffin as if to shake hands.

places.name: National Leprechaun Museum
places.description: A museum that cashes in on its infamous little people while teaching you a thing or two about the Irish fey.

places.name: Saint Audoen's Gate
places.description: The enchanting archway is the only surviving portal into Dublin's lost medieval wall.

places.name: Kilmainham Gaol
places.description: One of Ireland's most infamous prisons for rebels including men, women and children.

places.name: The Hungry Tree
places.description: An 80-year-old plane tree is devouring an iron bench at Ireland's oldest law school.

places.name: Kingship and Sacrifice
places.description: This permanent collection of preserved bodies taken from Irish bogs is one of the leading authorities on such morbid mummies.

places.name: John Kavanagh ('The Gravediggers')
places.description: This pub built its reputation pouring post-shift pints for employees of the cemetery with which it shares a wall.

places.name: Ancient Bog Butter
places.description: The block of aged dairy is probably still edible after 2,300 years in a peat bog.

places.name: Vintage Cocktail Club
places.description: A 1930s-style speakeasy with a secret doorway and one-of-a-kind cocktails.
